items.definition_en: Blood is the red liquid that flows in your body.
items.example_en: She cut her finger and a drop of blood appeared.

items.definition_en: Sweat is the salty liquid that comes from your skin when you are hot or nervous.
items.example_en: He wiped the sweat from his forehead after running.

items.definition_en: Tears are the drops of salty water that come from your eyes when you cry.
items.example_en: Her tears fell when she was feeling sad.

items.definition_en: Saliva is the watery liquid in your mouth.
items.example_en: He swallowed his saliva before speaking.

items.definition_en: Urine is the yellow liquid waste that comes from your body when you use the toilet.
items.example_en: Doctors sometimes test urine to check your health.

items.definition_en: Snot is the thick liquid from your nose, especially when you have a cold.
items.example_en: The child wiped snot from his nose with a tissue.

items.definition_en: Pus is a thick yellow or green liquid that comes from an infected cut or wound.
items.example_en: The cut on her arm was full of pus.

items.definition_en: Vomit is the food and liquid that comes out of your mouth when you are sick.
items.example_en: He cleaned the vomit from the carpet after his child was ill.

items.definition_en: Mucus is the slippery liquid in your nose and throat.
items.example_en: Mucus helps protect your nose from dust and germs.

items.definition_en: Phlegm is the thick liquid that you cough up from your lungs or throat.
items.example_en: She had a cough with lots of phlegm during her cold.

items.definition_en: Spit is the wet liquid in your mouth that you can throw out.
items.example_en: He spit on the ground after chewing gum.

items.definition_en: Breast milk is the milk produced by mothers for their babies.
items.example_en: Breast milk gives babies the nutrients they need.

items.definition_en: Sperm is the male cell needed to create a baby.
items.example_en: Sperm is part of the male reproductive system.

items.definition_en: Semen is the white liquid that carries sperm from the male body.
items.example_en: Doctors can test semen to check fertility.

items.definition_en: Earwax is the yellow substance that forms in your ears.
items.example_en: You should not clean earwax too deeply from your ears.

items.definition_en: Discharge is a liquid that can come from the body, often from a wound or infection.
items.example_en: The doctor checked the discharge to find the cause of the infection.

items.definition_en: Secretion is any liquid substance the body produces.
items.example_en: The skin makes an oil as a secretion.

items.definition_en: Bile is the greenish-yellow fluid made by the liver that helps digest food.
items.example_en: Bile is stored in the gallbladder until needed for digestion.

items.definition_en: Menstrual blood is the blood that comes from a woman's body during her period.
items.example_en: She used pads to absorb menstrual blood every month.

items.definition_en: Amniotic fluid is the liquid that surrounds a baby in the mother's womb.
items.example_en: Amniotic fluid helps protect the baby before birth.
